title: Chronal Calibration
description: Sum a list of frequency deltas, then find the first frequency seen twice when cycling through the list repeatedly.
writeup:
## Approach

The input is a list of signed integers representing frequency changes. Part 1 is a straightforward sum. Part 2 requires finding the first repeated cumulative sum, which may not occur until the list has been cycled through many times.

## Part 1

Simply sum all the deltas in one pass — Python's built-in `sum` with a generator handles this in a single line.

## Part 2

Keep a running frequency and a `set` of every value seen so far (starting with `0`). Cycle through the delta list repeatedly until the current frequency is already in the set — that's the first repeat. Using a set gives O(1) lookups, so the bottleneck is purely how many cycles are needed, which varies per input.
